text:
The letter of instruction shall be sent to the expert by [time and date].

The costs charged by the expert for preparing the report shall be met by the [applicant] / [respondent] / [parties equally] in the first instance [such costs to be capped at [amount]].

[Any questions shall be put to the expert by no later than 10 days after receipt of the report (in accordance with FPR 2010 r 25.10).]

[The expert shall respond to those questions by [time and date].]

[The costs charged by the expert for answering those questions shall be met by the [applicant] / [respondent] / [parties equally] / [party raising them] in the first instance.]

[Save as is expressly ordered by the court, the expert’s written report shall be admissible without the attendance at court of the expert.]

Property Values

(see alternative versions in order 1.1)

The value[s] of the [property] / [properties] listed below, namely [the family home name] / [other property name], shall be agreed if possible. In default of agreement by [time and date], the parties shall jointly instruct [an estate agent] / [a chartered surveyor] / [an appropriate expert] to act as a single joint expert and to provide a [valuation report] / [market appraisal] in respect of [the] / [each] property. [The same expert shall value each of the properties.] / [Separate experts shall value the separate properties.]

The identity of the expert[s] shall be [expert name[s]] / [chosen in accordance with paragraph [para number] above].

The letter of instruction [and [insert any other documents]] shall be sent to the expert[s] by [time and date].

The report[s] shall be sent to the court in electronic format and served on the parties simultaneously by [time and date].

Order to instruct a single joint expert Pensions on Divorce Expert (PODE)

(see alternative versions in order 1.1)

The parties shall jointly instruct [name of PODE] / [a suitable PODE, whose name shall be identified] as a single joint expert to provide a report in accordance with the attached letter of instruction, addressing:

metadata.source: Standard Family Orders — Volume 1: Financial Remedies (May 2026)
metadata.title: In the Family Court		Case No: [Case number]
